items.name: Tiger Walk
items.description: The most iconic Mizzou tradition. Incoming freshmen walk through the historic Columns toward Jesse Hall to officially begin their time at Mizzou. Marching Mizzou plays, the university president welcomes the new class, and returning students line the path to cheer. Seniors reverse the walk at Senior Sendoff in May.

items.name: Welcome Week
items.description: Five days of events welcoming new students to campus. Fall 2025 events included: Sweet Start at the Zou (ice cream social), First Roar (kickoff pep event at Southwest Lawn), Paint the M at Memorial Stadium, MizzouRec Fest, Midnight BBQ on Rollins Street, Flick on Francis (outdoor movie — Shrek on Francis Quad), Shakespeare's Pizza Party, and Tiger Walk.
items.frequency: Annually — the week before fall classes begin (Fall 2025: August 19–23)

items.name: Homecoming
items.description: Mizzou invented Homecoming in 1911 — it's the nation's first and still one of the largest. Week-long celebration includes a talent show, spirit rally at Traditions Plaza, and the Saturday parade through downtown Columbia followed by a home football game. Students also run the nation's largest homecoming blood drive (5,000+ units) and food drive (170,000+ lbs).
items.frequency: Annually in fall semester (Fall 2025: parade September 27, game vs. UMass — Tigers won 42–6)

items.name: Painting the Rock M
items.description: An annual Welcome Week tradition dating back to 1927. Freshmen paint a 90×95-foot 'M' made of leftover construction boulders. One of the most unique and photogenic traditions on campus.

items.name: MU Tigers Football
items.description: Home football games at Faurot Field / Memorial Stadium. The student section (Antlers) creates one of the loudest atmospheres in the SEC. Show your student ID for free or discounted student tickets. The 'MIZ-ZOU' chant echoes throughout every game.

items.name: Involvement Week
items.description: Held each semester, this is the best time to discover the 589+ student organizations on campus. Think of it as a sampler platter of everything Mizzou has to offer. Involvement Ambassadors are on hand to help match you with groups that fit your interests.
items.frequency: Beginning of fall and spring semesters
items.location: MU Student Center and campus common areas

items.name: Rub Francis's Nose
items.description: According to Mizzou legend, rubbing the nose of the bronze statue of David R. Francis (former MU president and Missouri governor) brings good luck on exams. The nose is noticeably shinier than the rest of the statue.
items.location: Northeast of Jesse Hall entrance, Francis Quadrangle

items.name: Mizzou Farmers Market
items.description: Local vendors sell fresh produce, baked goods, crafts, and plants on campus. A great way to support local Columbia businesses and pick up fresh food between classes.
